samples.xml_description:
<module id="asyn_fifo">
    <description>An asynchronous FIFO with configurable parameters DEPTH and WIDTH.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="wclk">Write clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rclk">Read clock signal.</input>
        <input id="wrstn">Write reset signal.</input>
        <input id="rrstn">Read reset signal.</input>
        <input id="winc">Write increment signal.</input>
        <input id="rinc">Read increment signal.</input>
        <input id="wdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Write data input.</input>
        <output id="wfull">Write full signal.</output>
        <output id="rempty">Read empty signal.</output>
        <output id="rdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Read data output.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <parameter id="WIDTH">FIFO data width.</parameter>
        <parameter id="DEPTH">FIFO depth.</parameter>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        // Define the dual-port RAM instance
        <instance id="dual_port_RAM" module_id="dual_port_RAM">
            <port id="wclk">wclk</port>
            <port id="rclk">rclk</port>
            <port id="wen">wen</port>
            <port id="ren">ren</port>
            <port id="waddr">waddr</port>
            <port id="raddr">raddr</port>
            <port id="wdata">wdata</port>
            <port id="rdata">rdata</port>
        </instance>
        
        // Define the write and read pointers
        <reg id="waddr_bin" width_description="[($clog2(DEPTH))-1:0]">Binary representation of the write pointer.</reg>
        <reg id="raddr_bin" width_description="[($clog2(DEPTH))-1:0]">Binary representation of the read pointer.</reg>
        
        // Define the write and read pointer buffers
        <reg id="wptr_buff" width_description="[($clog2(DEPTH))+1:0]">Buffer for the write pointer.</reg>
        <reg id="rptr_buff" width_description="[($clog2(DEPTH))+1:0]">Buffer for the read pointer.</reg>
        
        // Define the write and read pointer sync registers
        <reg id="wptr" width_description="[($clog2(DEPTH))+1:0]">Synchronized write pointer.</reg>
        <reg id="rptr" width_description="[($clog2(DEPTH))+1:0]">Synchronized read pointer.</reg>
        
        // Define the full and empty flags
        <wire id="wfull">Write full flag.</wire>
        <wire id="rempty">Read empty flag.</wire>
        
        // Assign the write and read addresses to the corresponding buffers
        <assign id="waddr" value="waddr_bin[$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0]" />
        <assign id="raddr" value="raddr_bin[$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0]" />
        
        // Assign the write and read data to the corresponding ports
        <assign id="wdata" value="wdata" />
        <assign id="rdata" value="rdata" />
        
        // Assign the write and read enables to the corresponding ports
        <assign id="wen" value="winc && !wfull" />
        <assign id="ren" value="rinc && !rempty" />
        
        // Convert the write pointer to Gray code
        <assign id="wptr[0]" value="waddr_bin[0]" />
        <assign id="wptr[1]" value="waddr_bin[1] ^ waddr_bin[0]" />
        <assign id="wptr[2]" value="waddr_bin[2] ^ waddr_bin[1]" />
        <assign id="wptr[3]" value="waddr_bin[3] ^ waddr_bin[2]" />
        <assign id="wptr[4]" value="waddr_bin[4] ^ waddr_bin[3]" />
        
        // Convert the read pointer to Gray code
        <assign id="rptr[0]" value="raddr_bin[0]" />
        <assign id="rptr[1]" value="raddr_bin[1] ^ raddr_bin[0]" />
        <assign id="rptr[2]" value="raddr_bin[2] ^ raddr_bin[1]" />
        <assign id="rptr[3]" value="raddr_bin[3] ^ raddr_bin[2]" />
        <assign id="rptr[4]" value="raddr_bin[4] ^ raddr_bin[3]" />
        
        // Synchronize the write pointer to the read clock domain
        <always id="sync_wptr" posedge="rclk" negedge="rrstn">
            if (!rrstn) begin
                rptr_buff <= 0;
            end else begin
                rptr_buff <= wptr;
            end
        end
        
        // Synchronize the read pointer to the write clock domain
        <always id="sync_rptr" posedge="wclk" negedge="wrstn">
            if (!wrstn) begin
                wptr_buff <= 0;
            end else begin
                wptr_buff <= rptr;
            end
        end
        
        // Compare the write pointer with the synchronized read pointer
        <assign id="wfull" value="(wptr == {~rptr_syn[4], rptr_syn[3:0]})" />
        
        // Compare the read pointer with the synchronized write pointer
        <assign id="rempty" value=(rptr == wptr_syn) />
        
        // Update the write pointer on the positive edge of the write clock
        <always id="update_wptr" posedge="wclk" negedge="wrstn">
            if (!wrstn) begin
                waddr_bin <= 0;
            end else begin
                if (winc && !wfull) begin
                    waddr_bin <= waddr_bin + 1;
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Update the read pointer on the positive edge of the read clock
        <always id="update_rptr" posedge="rclk" negedge="rrstn">
            if (!rrstn) begin
                raddr_bin <= 0;
            end else begin
                if (rinc && !rempty) begin
                    raddr_bin <= raddr_bin + 1;
                end
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    
    <implementation>
        // Implement the dual-port RAM
        dual_port_RAM #(
            .DEPTH(DEPTH),
            .WIDTH(WIDTH)
        ) dual_port_RAM_inst (
            .wclk(wclk),
            .rclk(rclk),
            .wen(wen),
            .ren(ren),
            .waddr(waddr),
            .raddr(raddr),
            .wdata(wdata),
            .rdata(rdata)
        );
        
        // Implement the write pointer synchronizer
        always @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn) begin
            if (!rrstn) begin
                rptr <= 0;
            end else begin
                rptr <= rptr_buff;
            end
        end
        
        // Implement the read pointer synchronizer
        always @(posedge wclk or negedge wrstn) begin
            if (!wrstn) begin
                wptr <= 0;
            end else begin
                wptr <= wptr_buff;
            end
        end
        
        // Implement the full and empty flags
        assign wfull = (wptr == {~rptr_syn[4], rptr_syn[3:0]});
        assign rempty = (rptr == wptr_syn);
        
        // Implement the write and read pointer buffers
        always @(posedge wclk or negedge wrstn) begin
            if (!wrstn) begin
                wptr_buff <= 0;
            end else begin
                wptr_buff <= wptr;
            end
        end
        
        always @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn) begin
            if (!rrstn) begin
                rptr_buff <= 0;
            end else begin
                rptr_buff <= rptr;
            end
        end
        
        // Implement the write and read addresses
        assign waddr = waddr_bin[$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0];
        assign raddr = raddr_bin[$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0];
        
        // Implement the write and read data
        assign wdata = wdata;
        assign rdata = rdata;
        
        // Implement the write and read enables
        assign wen = winc && !wfull;
        assign ren = rinc && !rempty;
        
        // Implement the Gray code conversion
        assign wptr[0] = waddr_bin[0];
        assign wptr[1] = waddr_bin[1] ^ waddr_bin[0];
        assign wptr[2] = waddr_bin[2] ^ waddr_bin[1];
        assign wptr[3] = waddr_bin[3] ^ waddr_bin[2];
        assign wptr[4] = waddr_bin[4] ^ waddr_bin[3];
        
        assign rptr[0] = raddr_bin[0];
        assign rptr[1] = raddr_bin[1] ^ raddr_bin[0];
        assign rptr[2] = raddr_bin[2] ^ raddr_bin[1];
        assign rptr[3] = raddr_bin[3] ^ raddr_bin[2];
        assign rptr[4] = raddr_bin[4] ^ raddr_bin[3];
        
        // Implement the comparison between the write and read pointers
        assign wfull = (wptr == {~rptr_syn[4], rptr_syn[3:0]});
        assign rempty = (rptr == wptr_syn);
        
        // Implement the update of the write and read pointers
        always @(posedge wclk or negedge wrstn) begin
            if (!wrstn) begin
                waddr_bin <= 0;
            end else begin
                if (winc && !wfull) begin
                    waddr_bin <= waddr_bin + 1;
                end
            end
        end
        
        always @(posedge rclk or negedge rrstn) begin
            if (!rrstn) begin
                raddr_bin <= 0;
            end else begin
                if (rinc && !rempty) begin
                    raddr_bin <= raddr_bin + 1;
                end
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>
